html:
Exclusive to:

The Queen of Moon Quay is an NPC exclusive to Don't Starve Together introduced in the The Curse of Moon Quay update. She lives on the Moon Quay along with Powder Monkeys, who reside in Monkey Huts.

Unlike the Pig King, who is only asleep during the Night and won't wake up until Daytime, the Queen of Moon Quay is constantly asleep until approached by a survivor or a Powder Monkey with a Banana, causing her to wake up. If all survivors are gone she will go back to sleep.

Contents

  • 1 Banana Exchange
  • 2 Right of Passage
  • 3 Quotes
  • 4 Monkey Bar
  • 5 Tips
  • 6 Gallery

Banana Exchange

The Queen's primary purpose is to rid the player of Accursed Trinkets in exchange for Bananas and Cooked Bananas. If the player has any Accursed Trinkets in their inventory and a Banana is given to the Queen, she will remove 4 Trinkets from that player's inventory. This is the only way to get rid of Accursed Trinkets, besides dying.

If the player does not have any Accursed Trinkets on them and gives a Banana to the Queen, she will instead drop a random Rare Blueprint from a small selection consisting of the Cannon Kit, Cannonball, Dock Kit, Dock Piling Kit and Moon Quay Beach Turf. She will give one of each for the first five Bananas and a random Blueprint for every Banana afterwards.

She can also be presented with other non-Banana Food Items and Trinkets, which she promptly rejects.

Right of Passage

Every time the Queen removes any of the player's Accursed Trinkets she will also activate the Right of Passage for 4 minutes. While the Right of Passage is active, Powder Monkeys on the Moon Quay become neutral to all players and will not attack players unless provoked.

The Right of Passage cannot be stacked and will always be reset to 4 minutes.

Quotes

Receiving a Banana

  • OOH, BENT GOLD.
  • FABULOUS QUALITY.
  • YOU'VE DONE WELL.

Receiving a Banana from a Powder Monkey

  • YOUR OFFERING PLEASES ME.
  • THE GREAT SKY BANANA SMILES THIS DAY.

Removing Accursed Trinkets

  • YOU WOULD RETURN SUCH A GIFT? SUIT YOURSELF.
  • YOU WISH TO RETURN TO THAT HIDEOUS FORM? VERY WELL.
  • I CAN'T FATHOM WHY YOU'D GIVE IT UP, BUT I'LL HONOR OUR ACCORD.

When waking

  • WHAT'S ALL THE FUSS NOW?
  • SPEAK. BUT MAKE IT QUICK.

When the player is not Wonkey (Prefix)

  • Ooo
  • Eee
  • Oee

When the player is not Wonkey (Suffix)

  • ki
  • k

Monkey Bar

The Monkey Bar is the structure that supports the Queen of Moon Quay's hammock. They perform a decorative role and cannot be interacted with. Crustashine will crawl up the bar.

Tips

  • The origin of Bananas used for trade is irrelevant; Bananas can be picked from the Banana Bushes around the Moon Quay, looted from Powder Monkeys or plundered from the Ruins and the Queen will accept them regardless.
  • While the player must receive 10 Accursed Trinkets to transform into Wonkey, they will not turn back to normal until all Accursed Trinkets are removed from their inventory.

Gallery

  • Concept art of Queen of Moon Quay shown in Rhymes With Play stream.
  • Concept art of Queen of Moon Quay shown in Rhymes With Play stream.
  • Concept art of Monkey bar shown in Rhymes With Play stream.

Exclusive to:
Queen of Moon Quay
Object Info
Perk
Gives ( ) and removes in exchange for and
Miscellaneous
"monkeyqueen"
“She looks like the top banana around here.”
–Wilson
“Being in charge looks like a pretty easy job.”
–Willow
“Hello hairy lady!”
–Wolfgang
“Queens don't usually scratch their backsides in public, do they Abigail?”
–Wendy
“HOW DARE YOU COMPARE THE MOON TO YOUR HIDEOUS SQUASHY BANANAS”
–WX-78
“How interesting, the apes seem to have a matriarchal society.”
–Wickerbottom
“She doesn't look like the one on the back of the loonie.”
–Woodie
“Hmph. I'd hardly call that a throne.”
–Maxwell
“Her eyes are cunning, she shöuld nöt be underestimated.”
–Wigfrid
“She looks pretty important.”
–Webber
“Bonjour, madame!”
–Warly
“Oh... she think Night Ball is friend hair?”
–Wormwood
“She must be one of the higher-ups.”
–Winona
“Her kingdom for a swing!”
–Wortox
“Who say you get to be Queen, flort?!”
–Wurt
“Hello ma'am! Do you need help getting down from there?”
–Walter
“And what do you have to say for all this skullduggery, hm?”
–Wanda
The Queen of Moon Quay is an NPC exclusive to Don't Starve Together introduced in the The Curse of Moon Quay update. She lives on the Moon Quay along with Powder Monkeys, who reside in Monkey Huts.
Unlike the Pig King, who is only asleep during the Night and won't wake up until Daytime, the Queen of Moon Quay is constantly asleep until approached by a survivor or a Powder Monkey with a Banana, causing her to wake up. If all survivors are gone she will go back to sleep.

1 Banana Exchange
2 Right of Passage
3 Quotes
4 Monkey Bar
5 Tips
6 Gallery

If the player does not have any Accursed Trinkets on them and gives a Banana to the Queen, she will instead drop a random Rare Blueprint from a small selection consisting of the Cannon Kit, Cannonball, Dock Kit, Dock Piling Kit and Moon Quay Beach Turf. She will give one of each for the first five Bananas and a random Blueprint for every Banana afterwards.
She can also be presented with other non-Banana Food Items and Trinkets, which she promptly rejects.

Every time the Queen removes any of the player's Accursed Trinkets she will also activate the Right of Passage for 4 minutes. While the Right of Passage is active, Powder Monkeys on the Moon Quay become neutral to all players and will not attack players unless provoked.
The Right of Passage cannot be stacked and will always be reset to 4 minutes.

Receiving a Banana
OOH, BENT GOLD.
FABULOUS QUALITY.
YOU'VE DONE WELL.
Receiving a Banana from a Powder Monkey
YOUR OFFERING PLEASES ME.
THE GREAT SKY BANANA SMILES THIS DAY.
Removing Accursed Trinkets
YOU WOULD RETURN SUCH A GIFT? SUIT YOURSELF.
YOU WISH TO RETURN TO THAT HIDEOUS FORM? VERY WELL.
I CAN'T FATHOM WHY YOU'D GIVE IT UP, BUT I'LL HONOR OUR ACCORD.
When waking
WHAT'S ALL THE FUSS NOW?
SPEAK. BUT MAKE IT QUICK.
When the player is not Wonkey (Prefix)
Ooo
Eee
Oee
When the player is not Wonkey (Suffix)
ki
k
Monkey Bar[edit | edit source]
Monkey Bar
Miscellaneous
"monkeypillar
“A real pillar of the community.”
–Wilson
“I wonder if it'll burn...”
–Willow
“Hmm. Is very strange tree.”
–Wolfgang
“Her kingdom is built on nothing but sand, and one day it will topple.”
–Wendy
“A PITIFUL STRUCTURE”
–WX-78
“These appear to be supporting the throne.”
–Wickerbottom
“Fake trees? It just ain't right.”
–Woodie
“How quaint.”
–Maxwell
“These trees did nöt gröw fröm the earth.”
–Wigfrid
“How far up does it go?”
–Webber
“They're tall enough to skewer the clouds!”
–Warly
“Metal trunk”
–Wormwood
“Looks shoddy, but it's stable somehow.”
–Winona
“Are these the pillars of monkey society?”
–Wortox
“Hey... it not real tree!”
–Wurt
“I wonder if I could climb all the way to the top...”
–Walter
“As pillars go, it's hardly noteworthy.”
–Wanda
The Monkey Bar is the structure that supports the Queen of Moon Quay's hammock. They perform a decorative role and cannot be interacted with. Crustashine will crawl up the bar.
Tips[edit | edit source]
The origin of Bananas used for trade is irrelevant; Bananas can be picked from the Banana Bushes around the Moon Quay, looted from Powder Monkeys or plundered from the Ruins and the Queen will accept them regardless.
While the player must receive 10 Accursed Trinkets to transform into Wonkey, they will not turn back to normal until all Accursed Trinkets are removed from their inventory.

The '''Queen of Moon Quay''' is an [[NPC]] exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'' introduced in the ''[[The Curse of Moon Quay]]'' update. She lives on the [[Moon Quay]] along with [[Powder Monkey|Powder Monkeys]], who reside in [[Monkey Hut]]s.

Unlike the [[Pig King/DST|Pig King]], who is only asleep during the [[Day-Night Cycle#Night|Night]] and won't wake up until [[Day-Night Cycle#Day|Daytime]], the Queen of Moon Quay is constantly asleep until approached by a survivor or a Powder Monkey with a [[Banana/DST|Banana]], causing her to wake up. If all survivors are gone she will go back to sleep.

==Banana Exchange ==
The Queen's primary purpose is to rid the player of [[Accursed Trinket]]s in exchange for [[Banana/DST|Bananas]] and [[Banana/DST#Cooked Banana|Cooked Bananas]]. If the player has any Accursed Trinkets in their inventory and a Banana is given to the Queen, she will remove 4 Trinkets from that player's inventory. This is the only way to get rid of Accursed Trinkets, besides dying. 

If the player does not have any Accursed Trinkets on them and gives a Banana to the Queen, she will instead drop a random [[Blueprint/DST#Rare Blueprint|Rare Blueprint]] from a small selection consisting of the [[Cannon Kit]], [[Cannonball]], [[Dock Kit]], [[Dock Piling Kit]] and [[Moon Quay Beach Turf]]. She will give one of each for the first five Bananas and a random Blueprint for every Banana afterwards.

She can also be presented with other non-Banana [[Food/DST|Food]] Items and [[Trinkets/DST|Trinkets]], which she promptly rejects.

== Right of Passage ==
Every time the Queen removes any of the player's Accursed Trinkets she will also activate the Right of Passage for 4 minutes. While the Right of Passage is active, Powder Monkeys on the Moon Quay become neutral to all players and will not attack players unless provoked. 

The Right of Passage cannot be stacked and will always be reset to 4 minutes.

The '''Monkey Bar''' is the structure that supports the Queen of Moon Quay's hammock. They perform a decorative role and cannot be interacted with. [[Crustashine]] will crawl up the bar.

=={{pic|32|Prototype}} Tips==

*The origin of Bananas used for trade is irrelevant; Bananas can be picked from the Banana Bushes around the Moon Quay, looted from Powder Monkeys or plundered from the [[Ruins/DST|Ruins]] and the Queen will accept them regardless.
*While the player must receive 10 Accursed Trinkets to transform into [[Wonkey]], they will not turn back to normal until all Accursed Trinkets are removed from their inventory.
